title: Introduction to Python
description: Your first steps with Python — printing, variables, and input.

tasks.title: Hello World
tasks.explainer:
Every programmer starts here. Use `print()` to show text on screen.

Put your message inside the brackets and wrap it in quotes:

```python
print('Hello, world!')
```

Run the code to see the output below. Then try changing the message!
tasks.starterCode:
# Write your first line of Python below
print('Hello, world!')

tasks.title: Variables
tasks.explainer:
A **variable** stores a piece of information so you can use it later.

```python
name = 'Jamie'
age = 12
print(name)
print(age)
```

Create a variable called `name` and set it to your name, then print a greeting using it.

tasks.title: Getting Input
tasks.explainer:
Use `input()` to ask the user to type something.

```python
name = input('What is your name? ')
print('Nice to meet you,', name)
```

Ask for the user's name, then print a greeting using their answer.
tasks.starterCode:
name = input('What is your name? ')
print('Nice to meet you,', name)

tasks.title: Numbers and Maths
tasks.explainer:
Python can do maths! Try these operators:

```python
print(10 + 3)   # addition
print(10 - 3)   # subtraction
print(10 * 3)   # multiplication
print(10 / 3)   # division
```

Ask the user for two numbers and print their **sum**. Use `int()` to convert the input to a number:

```python
a = int(input('First number: '))
```
tasks.starterCode:
a = int(input('First number: '))
b = int(input('Second number: '))
print(a + b)

tasks.title: Making Decisions
tasks.explainer:
An `if` statement lets your program choose what to do.

```python
age = int(input('How old are you? '))
if age >= 18:
    print('You can vote!')
else:
    print('Not old enough yet.')
```

Ask the user to enter a number. If it's greater than 10, print `Big number!`. Otherwise print `Small number!`.
